Hi, and welcome to Line Sheet. I=E2=80=99m back in Los Angeles. Like
many of you, I know a lot of people who have lost their homes, and even mor=
e who are fearful of what this week may bring. At the same time, those of u=
s who are lucky enough to feel safe at the moment have a chance to help tho=
se who don=E2=80=99t.

=C2=A0

Everyone processes terrible things differently. (My reflex is to go for a r=
un, which unfortunately I can=E2=80=99t do right now due to the smoke.) I=
=E2=80=99ve lived in Los Angeles for
nearly five years, arriving in July 2020 in an effort to escape another nat=
ural disaster of sorts: the pandemic. Unlike many others, we stayed, but th=
ere is no practical reason for me to live here. Our family is on the East C=
oast. My work is mostly in Paris. We love New York. And yet, the last thing=
 I want to do is leave.

=C2=A0

Today=E2=80=99s issue is mostly about things other than the fires, although=
 I do have some
big-picture thoughts on how fashion should be thinking about managing these=
 types of disasters. Elsewhere in the industry (at least outside Los Angele=

Last Paris Fashion Week, as rumors swirled that Loewe wunderkind
Jonathan Anderson was headed to Dior, Proenza Schouler founders and designe=
rs Jack McCollough and Lazaro Hernandez landed in Paris. It=E2=80=99s stand=
ard procedure for American designers who show in New York to spend time in =
Paris afterward; most store buyers=E2=80=94especially non-American ones=E2=
=80=94save the bulk of their budgets for Paris, and designers from around t=
he globe set up showrooms across the city to ensure they aren=E2=80=99t for=
gotten. But I heard Jack and
Laz, as their friends call them, were in town to interview for the Loewe jo=
b. Even back in October, folks were convinced that, after months=E2=80=94ye=
ars?=E2=80=94of speculation, a decision had been made, and that Jonathan An=
derson would leave the brand for Dior.=C2=A0

=C2=A0

The Proenza piece of it was less certain. It was just a little over a decad=
e ago that McCollough and Hernandez, who founded their brand right out of
Parsons in 2002 and have since become emblematic of a generation of America=
n designers, were themselves vying for the top job at Dior. In the interven=
ing years, however, McCollough and Hernandez have been through hell and bac=
k with Proenza Schouler, enduring a series of investor mismatches while the=
 larger industry was consumed by both consolidation and a profusion of inde=
pendent brands that rose and largely fell on a diet of cheap money and bad =
business models.=C2=A0

=C2=A0

By 2024, at last, the business had stabilized, with about $70 million in an=
nual sales ( https://puck.news/newsletter_content/sabato-hunger-cries-chery=
l-hines-at-bally-the-proenza-plans/?utm_campaign=3DLine+Sheet+-+SUBSCRIBERS=
+%281%2F13%2F25%29&utm_content=3DLine+Sheet+-+SUBSCRIBERS+%281%2F10%2F25%29=
&utm_medium=3Demail_action&utm_source=3Dcustomer.io&utm_term=3Df6c60617da42=
abd12a ), filling a niche for women who were interested in looking good but=
 disinterested in designer flash and increasingly out-of-reach prices. (Las=
t year, I wrote about their
under-$1,000 leather jeans, which felt like a bargain compared to Phoebe Ph=
ilo=E2=80=99s over-$5,000 version.) At independent boutiques across the U.S=
=2E, Proenza Schouler had become a numb=
er one or number two brand. For years,=
 Proenza was far too reliant on handbags, especially their hit cross-body, =
the PS1, and the business became vulnerable when the style fell out of favo=
r. Now, they were running a more diversified, less fad-driven business.=C2=
=A0

=C2=A0

And yet, while they had survived multiple reckonings and come out mostly st=
ronger for it=E2=80=94I=E2=80=99d say even humbled=E2=80=94the prospect tha=
t they would ever be appointed to a big house owned by Kering or LVMH seeme=
d diminished. They=E2=80=99d missed their chance, and were doing something =
different now. So when I heard they=E2=80=99d gone in to discuss Loewe with=
 Sidney Toledano & Co. at LVMH, I was unconvinced it would amount to anythi=
ng. In the months since,
other plausible names were floated, among them Luke and Lucie Meier, who de=
sign Jil Sander, and Francesco Risso of Marni fame. Wouldn=E2=80=99t it be =
interesting if it was Dario Vitale, the longtime Miu Miu designer director,=
 who is set to exit his post this month?

=C2=A0

But as the weeks went on, the argument for the Proenza boys grew stronger,
especially as Anderson=E2=80=99s appointment at Dior moved from speculative=
 to all but certain pending the press release. This past week, HSBC even pu=
t out a note remarking on Dior=E2=80=99s business challenges and suggesting=
 that the appointment of Anderson to the head of womenswear would put it ba=
ck on the right track.=C2=A0

=C2=A0

Also, last week, multiple sources told me that Hernandez and McCollough=E2=
=80=99s contract with LVMH had been signed, that
they would not show a Proenza Schouler runway collection at New York Fashio=
n Week in February, and that the appointment could be announced by the end =
of the month, probably after couture. I reached out to reps for Loewe and P=
roenza Schouler, and also Hernandez and McCollough directly, for comment, b=
ut have yet to receive a response.=C2=A0

The Feels
---------

At the moment, I don=E2=80=99t know any further details about Hernandez and
McCollough=E2=80=99s deal with LVMH, and whether the decision to bow out of=
 New York Fashion Week was simply a matter of time management, or a busines=
s change at the brand, whose current investors are owed an exit. There=E2=
=80=99s always a chance that LVMH will take a stake in the business and res=
tructure it, but the group has essentially stopped investing in designer-ow=
ned brands as a way to acqui-hire talent, ever since acquiring a stake in J=
=2EW. Anderson in 2013. It=E2=80=99s just too messy.

=C2=A0

In the fashion community, the prospect of Hernandez and McCollough taking o=
ver Loewe has been met with=E2=80=A6 emotions. Particularly in America, whe=
re they continue to be adored and admired by editors and buyers who see the=
ir business challenges as representative of the struggles of young designer=
s in the U.S. Proenza had everything=E2=80=94talent, Hollywood good looks, =
a hit handbag, Anna
Wintour=E2=80=94and they still didn=E2=80=99t make it all the way. If they=
=E2=80=99re given another chance with Loewe, or so this logic goes, it woul=
d be an indication that the success formula does indeed work, even if the p=
ath is longer and more circuitous than anticipated.=C2=A0

=C2=A0

In Europe, however, there is some apprehension about the pair taking over a=
t Loewe. On the positive side, Hernandez
and McCollough are respected there, which is no small thing. Most Europeans=
 dismiss Americans who haven=E2=80=99t led a European house, but not the Pr=
oenza boys. I remember a snobby German editor looking at me seriously and s=
aying, =E2=80=9CThey=E2=80=99re real designers.=E2=80=9D

=C2=A0

That said, there is skepticism that Hernandez and McCollough will be able t=
o bring the requisite newness to Loewe required to keep the consumer=E2=80=
=99s interest
piqued. There=E2=80=99s no doubt they can take Anderson=E2=80=99s foundatio=
n and bring commercial polish to it. How they=E2=80=99ll innovate is anothe=
r question. But overall, the vibe is: Good for them.=C2=A0

Lingering
Questions
---------

As for Anderson at Dior, there are still questions about what
exactly that match will look like. It=E2=80=99s understood that Anderson ha=
s so far resisted a big appointment where he wouldn=E2=80=99t be designing =
both men=E2=80=99s and women=E2=80=99s. He=E2=80=99s particular and intenti=
onal about how he does things, just like Hedi Slimane. At Loewe, he control=
led everything, from the social media output to the runway set. Even if he =
is designing men=E2=80=99s and women=E2=80=99s at Dior=E2=80=94and it seems=
 increasingly likely that he=E2=80=99ll be responsible for women=E2=80=99s =
only=E2=80=94the machine behind him
will make it nearly impossible to evolve the marketing, store design, and o=
ther consumer experiences in a meaningful way=E2=80=A6 at least not quickly=
=2E The good news, for us, is that Ande=
rson seems to thrive in difficult situ=
ations.

=C2=A0

No matter the specifics of the role, the Anderson appointment will set off =

nderson replacing Maria
Grazia Chiuri, or Maria Grazia Chiuri and Kim Jones? Is Delphine Arnault st=
aying at Dior as C.E.O.? If not, is she headed to the Fashion Group? Finall=
y, what becomes of Chiuri?=C2=A0

=C2=A0

Given the tremendous amount she has contributed to LVMH over the past seven=
 years, I expect that Bernard Arnault will give Chiuri what she wants, and =
that the
Rome resort show schedule for May will go on, with Anderson making his debu=
t either at couture in July or ready-to-wear in October, head-to-head with =
Matthieu Blazy at Chanel. And remember, Fendi, which celebrates its 100th a=
nniversary this year, is currently without a designer. It just so happens t=
o be headquartered in Rome=E2=80=A6 Chiuri=E2=80=99s hometown.=C2=A0

=3D"margin:0;margin-bottom:0px;">Hi, and welcome to Line Sheet. I=E2=80=99m=
 back in Los Angeles. Like

s who are lucky enough to feel safe at the moment have a chance to help tho=
se who don=E2=80=99t.</p><p style=3D"margin:0;margin-bottom:0px;">=C2=A0</p=
><p style=3D"margin:0;margin-bottom:0px;">Everyone processes terrible thing=
s differently. (My reflex is to go for a run, which unfortunately I can=E2=
=80=99t do right now due to the smoke.) I=E2=80=99ve lived in Los Angeles f=
or
nearly five years, arriving in July 2020 in an effort to escape another nat=

 Since the end of last year, when word got out that <strong>Miuccia</strong=

strong> would be leaving Miu Miu this month, folks in Europe have been tryi=

und is that he has been <em>in talks</em> with Versace.=C2=A0<br/><br/>As
for what Vitale was (or is) talking to them about? It remains unclear wheth=

 <strong>Versace</strong>, replacing her altogether, or joining as co-creat=
ive director (<strong>Raf</strong>-and-Miuccia style). My understanding is =

collection, set to debut at Milan Fashion Week in February. A rep for Versa=
ce declined to comment on the matter.=C2=A0<br/><br/>The Vitale murmurs exp=
lain why I was intrigued by last week=E2=80=99s leak that the Prada Group w=

 most importantly, they would know how to scale the business without furthe=

the details are what matter. Meanwhile, let=E2=80=99s hope a vulture firm d=

><em>The force majeure clause only gets you so far</em>: I haven=E2=80=99t =
been online as much this past week, prioritizing friends and family as many=
 people around me scramble to figure out where to go for the next few weeks=
, months, and even years. However, I=E2=80=99ve felt a tremendous amount of=
 <em>d=C3=A9j=C3=A0
vu</em> back to the early days of Covid, regarding what small businesses, a=
nd retailers of every size, are now facing=E2=80=94store closures, loss of =
merchandise, fulfillment breakdowns, challenges with employees being able t=
o make it to work, and having enough work for them if they do, etcetera. Un=
like the pandemic, this is a regional trauma, but it underscores that most =
fashion businesses=E2=80=94with razor-thin margins, shallow balance sheets,=

prepared for this sort of loss. This tragedy serves as another reminder for=
 businesses to remodel their contingency plans for improbable events where =
they are impacted directly or indirectly. Unfortunately, these sorts of dis=
asters are only going to become more frequent.</li></ul></div>

px;line-height:150%;text-align:left;mso-line-height-alt:25.5px;"><p style=
=3D"margin:0;margin-bottom:0px;">Last Paris Fashion Week, as rumors swirled=
 that Loewe wunderkind
<strong>Jonathan Anderson</strong> was headed to Dior, Proenza Schouler fou=
nders and designers <strong>Jack McCollough</strong> and <strong>Lazaro Her=
nandez</strong> landed in Paris. It=E2=80=99s standard procedure for Americ=
an designers who show in New York to spend time in Paris afterward; most st=
ore buyers=E2=80=94especially non-American ones=E2=80=94save the bulk of th=
eir budgets for Paris, and designers from around the globe set up showrooms=
 across the city to ensure they aren=E2=80=99t forgotten. But I heard Jack =
and
Laz, as their friends call them, were in town to interview for the Loewe jo=
b. Even back in October, folks were convinced that, after months=E2=80=94ye=
ars?=E2=80=94of speculation, a decision had been made, and that Jonathan An=
derson would leave the brand for Dior.=C2=A0</p><p style=3D"margin:0;margin=
-bottom:0px;">=C2=A0</p><p style=3D"margin:0;margin-bottom:0px;">The Proenz=
a piece of it was less certain. It was just a little over a decade ago that=
 McCollough and Hernandez, who founded their brand right out of
Parsons in 2002 and have since become emblematic of a generation of America=
n designers, were <em>themselves</em> vying for the top job at Dior. In the=
 intervening years, however, McCollough and Hernandez have been through hel=
l and back with Proenza Schouler, enduring a series of investor mismatches =
while the larger industry was consumed by both consolidation and a profusio=
n of independent brands that rose and largely fell on a diet of cheap money=
 and bad business models.=C2=A0</p><p style=3D"margin:0;margin-bottom:0px;"=
>=C2=A0</p><p style=3D"margin:0;margin-bottom:0px;">By 2024, at last, the b=
usiness had stabilized, with about <a href=3D"https://email.puck.news/e/c/e=
yJlbWFpbF9pZCI6ImRnVDJ4Z1lEQUt2UktxclJLZ0dVWWJ3Z2dzVDVvWk5nM21LaG5YMD0iLCJo=
cmVmIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly9wdWNrLm5ld3MvbmV3c2xldHRlcl9jb250ZW50L3NhYmF0by1odW5nZXI=
tY3JpZXMtY2hlcnlsLWhpbmVzLWF0LWJhbGx5LXRoZS1wcm9lbnphLXBsYW5zLz91dG1fY2FtcG=
FpZ249TGluZStTaGVldCstK1NVQlNDUklCRVJTKyUyODElMkYxMyUyRjI1JTI5XHUwMDI2dXRtX=

dXRtX21lZGl1bT1lbWFpbF9hY3Rpb25cdTAwMjZ1dG1fc291cmNlPWN1c3RvbWVyLmlvXHUwMDI=
2dXRtX3Rlcm09ZjZjNjA2MTdkYTQyYWJkMTJhIiwiaW50ZXJuYWwiOiJmNmM2MDYxN2RhNDJhYm=
QxMmEiLCJsaW5rX2lkIjoxMTI0NzY4fQ/1c90154ffd2a27371d5174ca695a1d1e06a0a93e6b=
224e190528eb70193ba5a2" style=3D"text-decoration:underline;color:#d40000;">=
$70 million in annual sales</a>, filling a niche for women who were interes=
ted in looking good but disinterested in designer flash and increasingly ou=
t-of-reach prices. (Last year, I wrote about their
under-$1,000 leather jeans, which felt like a bargain compared to Phoebe Ph=
ilo=E2=80=99s over-$5,000 version.) At independent boutiques across the U.S=
=2E, Proenza Schouler had become a numb=
er one or number two brand. For years,=
 Proenza was far too reliant on handbags, especially their hit cross-body, =
the PS1, and the business became vulnerable when the style fell out of favo=
r. Now, they were running a more diversified, less fad-driven business.=C2=
=A0</p><p style=3D"margin:0;margin-bottom:0px;">=C2=A0</p><p style=3D"margi=
n:0;margin-bottom:0px;">And yet, while they had survived multiple reckoning=
s and come out mostly stronger for it=E2=80=94I=E2=80=99d say even <em>humb=
led</em>=E2=80=94the prospect that they would ever be appointed to a big ho=
use owned by Kering or LVMH seemed diminished. They=E2=80=99d missed their =
chance, and were doing something different now. So when I heard they=E2=80=
=99d gone in to discuss Loewe with <strong>Sidney Toledano</strong> & C=
o. at LVMH, I was unconvinced it would amount to anything. In the months si=
nce,
other plausible names were floated, among them <strong>Luke</strong> and <s=
trong>Lucie Meier</strong>, who design Jil Sander, and <strong>Francesco Ri=
sso</strong> of Marni fame. Wouldn=E2=80=99t it be interesting if it was <s=
trong>Dario Vitale</strong>, the longtime Miu Miu designer director, who is=
 set to exit his post this month?</p><p style=3D"margin:0;margin-bottom:0px=
;">=C2=A0</p><p style=3D"margin:0;margin-bottom:0px;">But as the weeks went=
 on, the argument for the Proenza boys grew stronger,
especially as Anderson=E2=80=99s appointment at Dior moved from speculative=
 to all but certain pending the press release. This past week, HSBC even pu=
t out a note remarking on Dior=E2=80=99s business challenges and suggesting=
 that the appointment of Anderson to the head of womenswear would put it ba=
ck on the right track.=C2=A0</p><p style=3D"margin:0;margin-bottom:0px;">=
=C2=A0</p><p style=3D"margin:0;">Also, last week, multiple sources told me =
that Hernandez and McCollough=E2=80=99s contract with LVMH had been signed,=
 that
they would not show a Proenza Schouler runway collection at New York Fashio=
n Week in February, and that the appointment could be announced by the end =
of the month, probably after couture. I reached out to reps for Loewe and P=
roenza Schouler, and also Hernandez and McCollough directly, for comment, b=
ut have yet to receive a response.=C2=A0</p></div>

line-height:150%;text-align:left;mso-line-height-alt:25.5px;"><p style=3D"m=
argin:0;margin-bottom:0px;">At the moment, I don=E2=80=99t know any further=
 details about Hernandez and
McCollough=E2=80=99s deal with LVMH, and whether the decision to bow out of=
 New York Fashion Week was simply a matter of time management, or a busines=
s change at the brand, whose current investors are owed an exit. There=E2=
=80=99s always a chance that LVMH will take a stake in the business and res=
tructure it, but the group has essentially stopped investing in designer-ow=
ned brands as a way to acqui-hire talent, ever since acquiring a stake in J=
=2EW. Anderson in 2013. It=E2=80=99s just=20=
too messy.</p><p style=3D"margin:0;
margin-bottom:0px;">=C2=A0</p><p style=3D"margin:0;margin-bottom:0px;">In t=
he fashion community, the prospect of Hernandez and McCollough taking over =
Loewe has been met with=E2=80=A6 <em>emotions</em>. Particularly in America=
, where they continue to be adored and admired by editors and buyers who se=
e their business challenges as representative of the struggles of young des=
igners in the U.S. Proenza had everything=E2=80=94talent, Hollywood good lo=
oks, a hit handbag, <strong>Anna</strong>
<strong>Wintour</strong>=E2=80=94and they still didn=E2=80=99t make it all =
the way. If they=E2=80=99re given another chance with Loewe, or so this log=
ic goes, it would be an indication that the success formula<em> does </em>i=
ndeed work, even if the path is longer and more circuitous than anticipated=
=2E=C2=A0</p><p style=3D"margin:0;margi=
n-bottom:0px;">=C2=A0</p><p style=3D"m=
argin:0;margin-bottom:0px;">In Europe, however, there is <em>some</em> appr=
ehension about the pair taking over at Loewe. On the positive side, Hernand=
ez
and McCollough are respected there, which is no small thing. Most Europeans=
 dismiss Americans who haven=E2=80=99t led a European house, but not the Pr=
oenza boys. I remember a snobby German editor looking at me seriously and s=
aying, =E2=80=9CThey=E2=80=99re<em> real</em> designers.=E2=80=9D</p><p sty=
le=3D"margin:0;margin-bottom:0px;">=C2=A0</p><p style=3D"margin:0;">That sa=
id, there is skepticism that Hernandez and McCollough will be able to bring=
 the requisite <em>newness</em> to Loewe required to keep the consumer=E2=
=80=99s interest
piqued. There=E2=80=99s no doubt they can take Anderson=E2=80=99s foundatio=
n and bring commercial polish to it. How they=E2=80=99ll innovate is anothe=
r question. But overall, the vibe is: <em>Good for them</em>.=C2=A0</p></di=
v>
